notes: The primary platform for which Marathon's Endurance product provided high availability. Deck argues NT is gaining mission-critical market share despite reliability concerns.
Marathon's flagship product providing continuous availability for NT servers via lockstep dual-processor architecture.
Named competitor; Microsoft's own two-node failover clustering for NT 4.0. Kastner implies it is weak relative to Endurance.
Competing high-availability platform; referred to implicitly as 'Stratus Radio' suggesting niche/overpriced positioning in the deck.
Competing proprietary fault-tolerant platform; expensive and UNIX/proprietary-oriented relative to NT market.
Identified as transitioning to Supply Chain Management; cited as a key workload driver for high-availability NT servers.
Cited as automating Sales & Marketing functions; listed as an Endurance target workload.
Described as 'the usual mission-critical OLTP applications'; a core workload justification for fault-tolerant NT.
Cited as a key emerging workload for high-availability servers; ISPs named as a target vertical.
Listed as a growth workload requiring high availability; reflects mid-1998 e-commerce boom expectations.
Cited as a target workload: higher service at lower costs via automation.
